title: Genomic DNA extraction from Mycobacterium avium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4u2b1","text":"Mycobacterium avium subsp. hominissuis (MAH) is one of the most important agents causing non-tuberculosis mycobacterial infection in human and pigs. Genome analysis on MAH of human isolates has been proceeding, however, those of pigs are limited despite its potential source of infection to human. Here, we isolated  MAH from pig lymph nodes or livers and obtained their genomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":26,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"9vl8c","text":"TE: Tris-EDTA Buffer; 10 mM Tris-Cl (pH 8.0), 1 mM EDTA; sterilize by autoclaving.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6691s","text":"Phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol; 25:24:1","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Suspend colonies on 7H11 agar into 300 µl of TE.
Discard supernatant, followed by suspension in 300 µl of acetone.
Discard acetone, followed by suspension in 200 µl of TE.
Add lysozyme at the final concentration of 100 µg/ml.
Add proteinase K at the final concentration of 0.2 mg/ml.
Add equal volume of phenol/chloroform/isoamyl-alcohol and shake vigorously.
Transfer aquatus phase to a new tube.
Remove supernatant and wash with 70% ethanol.
Suspended in 400 µl of TE, followed by adding RNase at the final concentration of 100 µg/ml.
creator: Tetsuya Komatsu
